method_atom: An exact duality between two ostensibly distinct descriptions of the same underlying structure, established by demonstrating that an entire family of observables — labelled by arbitrary representations of a symmetry — agrees order-by-order in a large expansion parameter, together with a reformulation of a topological invariant as a generating function of new integer-valued invariants that enumerate a discrete spectrum (and internal quantum numbers such as spin) of extended excitations, further tied to effective terms generated by summing over instanton contributions.
object_atom: Wilson-loop knot invariants of knots on the three-sphere in large-N Chern-Simons gauge theory, matched to the topological string on the resolved conifold geometry, where the integer invariants count M2-branes ending on M5-branes and connect to superpotential terms from worldsheet instantons in four-dimensional N=1 supersymmetric theories.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is a large-parameter duality verified by all-orders observable matching plus a recasting of an invariant into integer counting invariants of a spectrum, while the knots, Chern-Simons/topological-string setup, conifold, and branes are the domain-specific object.
